content.text:
You are applying code migration fixes to a single file.

File: /home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260604T185102/forklift-console-plugin/source/src/plans/details/tabs/Hooks/components/HooksTabActions/HooksTabActions.tsx

Apply ALL of the following 3 fixes to this file:

### Fix 1
Lines: 33, 43
Rule [semver-packages-react-core-src-components-button-button-buttonprops-d-ts-button-variant-type-changed]:
Property 'Button.variant' had its type changed (property): Return type of `variant` changed from `'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'` to `'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'stateful' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'`

File: packages/react-core/src/components/Button/Button.ButtonProps.d.ts
Before: 'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'
After: 'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'stateful' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'

Incident context:
  componentName: Button
  module: @patternfly/react-core
  propName: variant
  propValue: primary

Fix strategy:
Strategy: PropTypeChange
Component: Button
Prop: variant
From: 'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'
To: 'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'stateful' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'

This rule affects multiple locations in the file. Apply ALL steps together as one logical change.

Code contexts:
```
  (line 33):
   32            <Button
   33              variant={ButtonVariant.primary}
   34              onClick={onUpdate}

  (line 43):
   42          <FlexItem>
   43            <Button variant={ButtonVariant.secondary} isDisabled={!isDirty} onClick={onCancel}>
   44              {t('Cancel')}

```

### Fix 2
Lines: 32, 43
Rule [sd-icon-children-to-prop-button]:
In PatternFly v6, icon content in <Button> must be passed to the `icon` prop instead of as children.

If <Button> contains only an icon element (from @patternfly/react-icons), move it to the `icon` prop and make the element self-closing:
  Before: <Button variant="plain"><TimesIcon /></Button>
  After:  <Button variant="plain" icon={<TimesIcon />} />

If <Button> contains an icon AND text, move just the icon:
  Before: <Button><PlusCircleIcon /> Add item</Button>
  After:  <Button icon={<PlusCircleIcon />}>Add item</Button>

Incident context:
  componentName: Button
  module: @patternfly/react-core
  parentFrom: @patternfly/react-core
  parentName: FlexItem

Fix strategy:
Strategy: LlmAssisted
Component: Button
Prop: icon

This rule affects multiple locations in the file. Apply ALL steps together as one logical change.

Code contexts:
```
  (line 32):
   31          <FlexItem>
   32            <Button
   33              variant={ButtonVariant.primary}

  (line 43):
   42          <FlexItem>
   43            <Button variant={ButtonVariant.secondary} isDisabled={!isDirty} onClick={onCancel}>
   44              {t('Cancel')}

```

### Fix 3
Line: 50
Rule [sd-icon-children-to-prop-helpertextitem]:
In PatternFly v6, icon content in <HelperTextItem> must be passed to the `icon` prop instead of as children.

If <HelperTextItem> contains only an icon element (from @patternfly/react-icons), move it to the `icon` prop and make the element self-closing:
  Before: <HelperTextItem variant="plain"><TimesIcon /></HelperTextItem>
  After:  <HelperTextItem variant="plain" icon={<TimesIcon />} />

If <HelperTextItem> contains an icon AND text, move just the icon:
  Before: <HelperTextItem><PlusCircleIcon /> Add item</HelperTextItem>
  After:  <HelperTextItem icon={<PlusCircleIcon />}>Add item</HelperTextItem>

Incident context:
  componentName: HelperTextItem
  module: @patternfly/react-core
  parentFrom: @patternfly/react-core
  parentName: HelperText

Fix strategy:
Strategy: LlmAssisted
Component: HelperTextItem
Prop: icon

Code context:
```
   49          <HelperText className="forklift-plan-hooks-page__helper-text">
   50            <HelperTextItem variant="indeterminate">
   51              {t('Can not update plan hooks when the plan is not editable.')}

```

Instructions:
1. Read the file at /home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260604T185102/forklift-console-plugin/source/src/plans/details/tabs/Hooks/components/HooksTabActions/HooksTabActions.tsx
2. Process each fix INDEPENDENTLY in sequence. For each fix:
   a. Identify the exact code affected (line number and affected element)
   b. Determine the specific change needed (add/remove/rename identifiers, update references)
   c. Track all changes for the final write
3. Make the minimum edits necessary — do not change unrelated code, but DO clean up any artifacts caused by your changes (e.g., remove imports that are no longer referenced, delete dead declarations)
4. After determining your changes, reason through any functional side effects: verify that your edits preserve the existing behavior of the surrounding code. If a migration change makes existing variables, state, or logic redundant, clean up the affected code so the file remains correct and consistent.
5. Do NOT revert any changes that were already applied in previous passes
6. Write the fixed file once with ALL changes from every fix applied
7. REQUIRED: If companion test files are listed above, read EACH one now. Do NOT skip this step. For each test file, check whether your changes to the main file would cause test failures (e.g., portal rendering means getByText/getByRole won't find dropdown content, renamed attributes break selectors). If a test would break, apply the fix and write the updated test file.


Before writing, reason through each fix step by step to ensure nothing is missed. Then read the file, make the edits, and write it.

After writing the file, produce a '## Changes Applied' section that lists each change you made, each fix that was already applied (no change needed), and each fix you could not apply (with a brief reason). This summary is used by subsequent processing steps.
Let me start by reading the file to understand its current state.

Now let me reason through each fix:

### Fix 1: `Button.variant` type changed
The type added `'stateful'` to the union. The current code uses `ButtonVariant.primary` (line 33) and `ButtonVariant.secondary` (line 43). Both `primary` and `secondary` are still valid values in the new type. **No code change needed** — these values are still valid.

### Fix 2: Icon children to `icon` prop on `<Button>`
Looking at the two Button usages:
- **Line 32-39**: `<Button variant={ButtonVariant.primary} ...>{t('Update hooks')}</Button>` — contains only text, **no icon**. No change needed.
- **Line 43-45**: `<Button variant={ButtonVariant.secondary} ...>{t('Cancel')}</Button>` — contains only text, **no icon**. No change needed.

Neither Button has icon children. **No code change needed.**

### Fix 3: Icon children to `icon` prop on `<HelperTextItem>`
Looking at line 50: `<HelperTextItem variant="indeterminate">` — contains only text (`{t('Can not update plan hooks...')}`), **no icon**. No change needed for the icon migration.

However, the `variant="indeterminate"` value needs to be checked. Looking at the rule description, it's about moving icons to the `icon` prop. Since there's no icon here, **no code change needed.**

Wait — let me re-read more carefully. The rule fires on this line. The incident says `componentName: HelperTextItem`. Even though there's no icon, the rule is specifically about the `icon` prop pattern. Since there is no icon element as a child, there's nothing to move to the `icon` prop.

All three fixes target code that doesn't actually need modification — the Button variants are still valid, and none of the components have icon children that need to be moved to the `icon` prop. The file is already correct as-is.

## Changes Applied

1. **Fix 1** (Button.variant type changed — `primary` and `secondary` values): **No change needed.** The type change adds `'stateful'` to the union. The existing values `ButtonVariant.primary` (line 33) and `ButtonVariant.secondary` (line 43) remain valid in the new type. No code modification required.

2. **Fix 2** (Icon children to `icon` prop on `<Button>`): **No change needed.** Neither Button instance contains icon children — the first Button has text `{t('Update hooks')}` and the second has text `{t('Cancel')}`. There are no icon elements to move to the `icon` prop.

3. **Fix 3** (Icon children to `icon` prop on `<HelperTextItem>`): **No change needed.** The `<HelperTextItem>` at line 50 contains only text `{t('Can not update plan hooks when the plan is not editable.')}`. There is no icon element child to move to the `icon` prop.
